short_description: Lithium battery transport regulations apply
description: Lithium batteries shipped with this product must comply with China's dangerous goods transport regulations, including UN38.3 testing and the "One Book, One Label" requirements.
source_citation: Civil Aviation Administration of China Lithium Battery Transport Regulations
exemptions: Batteries under 20Wh for standalone or 100Wh for equipment-contained may qualify for simplified transport documentation.
risk_scenarios: Used, damaged, or prototype batteries are subject to stricter transport regulations (UN 3480/3481). Air transport of lithium batteries without UN38.3 test certification can result in shipment rejection and potential blacklisting by carriers. Temperature-sensitive battery storage during transport may require additional documentation.

short_description: Lithium battery transport regulations for power banks
description: Power banks and standalone lithium batteries are subject to additional dangerous goods transport regulations, including UN38.3 testing, and in some cases require special declaration and packaging. Restrictions apply to battery capacity and quantity per shipment.
source_citation: Announcement of MOT on Dangerous Goods Transport (2025)

short_description: Battery transport safety for e-scooters and e-bikes
description: Electric scooters, e-bikes, self-balancing hoverboards, and similar personal mobility devices with large lithium batteries are subject to strict dangerous goods transport regulations (UN3171 for battery-powered vehicles). Many are restricted or prohibited in certain transport modes due to fire risk.
source_citation: Announcement of MOT on Dangerous Goods Transport; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; IMDG Code

short_description: China Compulsory Certification required
description: This product falls under "Audio & Video Equipment" (Class 08) in the China Compulsory Certification (CCC) catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on Adjusting the CCC Product Catalog (2023)
exemptions: Products without wireless functionality may not require SRRC; battery-powered devices under 100Wh may have simplified transport requirements.
risk_scenarios: If the smart speaker contains far-field voice collection (e.g., 4+ microphone array), it may trigger additional data security review under China's Personal Information Protection Law. Microphone arrays capable of spatial audio capture may raise dual-use concerns if the sensitivity exceeds certain thresholds.

short_description: China Compulsory Certification required
description: This product falls under "Information Technology Equipment" (Class 09) in the CCC catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on Adjusting the CCC Product Catalog (2023)
exemptions: Processors sold as standalone components (not integrated into a finished system) may not require CCC certification. Engineering samples and development boards for R&D purposes are generally exempt. Processors embedded in equipment that already has CCC certification do not need separate CCC.
risk_scenarios: If the CPU is integrated into a server or computing system for export, the entire system may require CCC certification. High-performance processors used in AI training clusters or supercomputing systems may trigger dual-use export controls in addition to CCC requirements. Customs may require proof of end-use for processors above certain performance thresholds.

short_description: China Compulsory Certification required
description: This product falls under "Telecommunication Terminal Equipment" (Class 16) in the CCC catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on Adjusting the CCC Product Catalog (2023)

short_description: China Compulsory Certification required for power banks
description: Power banks and portable power sources fall under "Power Supply Equipment" in the CCC catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: China Compulsory Certification required for home appliances
description: Smart home devices and small appliances fall under "Household and Similar Electrical Appliances" in the CCC catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for vehicle electronics
description: Car dash cameras, vehicle GPS trackers, and in-vehicle electronics may fall under CCC certification categories for IT or AV equipment.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for gaming consoles and accessories
description: Gaming consoles and accessories may fall under IT equipment (Class 09) or AV equipment (Class 08) in the CCC catalog.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: IP/network cameras require CCC, SRRC, encryption, and dual-use screening
description: IP cameras, network cameras, CCTV cameras, smart security cameras, and camera-plus-network-storage products exported from China should be screened for CCC/product-safety applicability, SRRC if they contain Wi-Fi/Bluetooth/radio modules, commercial encryption controls if they transmit or store encrypted video, and dual-use triggers if they include thermal imaging, night vision, AI tracking, or surveillance-grade functions.
source_citation: CNCA CCC Product Catalog; MIIT SRRC Radio Equipment Approval; Commercial Encryption Import/Export Control Lists
exemptions: Simple wired cameras without wireless, encryption, night vision, thermal imaging, or AI tracking functions may only require ordinary product classification review.
risk_scenarios: Products combining camera capture, network transmission, storage encryption, facial or object recognition, infrared or night vision, or long-range wireless modules may trigger multiple reviews instead of a single CCC check.

short_description: CCC certification for medical devices
description: Certain home-use medical devices require CCC certification in addition to medical device registration with NMPA.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for projectors
description: Projectors fall under "IT Equipment" (Class 09) or "AV Equipment" (Class 08) in the CCC catalog depending on specifications.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for power adapters and chargers
description: Power adapters, chargers, and USB power supplies require CCC certification under the "Power Supply Equipment" category.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for storage devices
description: External hard drives, SSDs, and USB flash drives may require CCC certification under IT equipment rules.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)
exemptions: Bare NAND flash chips and raw memory components sold to system integrators are generally exempt from CCC as standalone components. Memory products embedded in CCC-certified end products do not require separate CCC certification.
risk_scenarios: SSDs and finished storage products must have CCC certification. Enterprise-grade SSDs with encryption capabilities may trigger additional commercial encryption controls. Memory products with capacities above certain thresholds destined for data centers in sensitive regions may face enhanced end-use scrutiny.

short_description: CCC certification for keyboards and peripherals
description: Computer keyboards, mice, and other input peripherals may require CCC certification under IT equipment rules.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for VR/AR headsets and smart glasses
description: VR headsets, AR glasses, and smart glasses may fall under IT equipment (Class 09) or AV equipment (Class 08) in the CCC catalog, depending on product specifications and connectivity features.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: CCC certification for e-cigarettes and vaping devices
description: Electronic cigarettes, vaping devices, and related electronic components are subject to special regulatory oversight in China. The electronic components and chargers may fall under CCC certification requirements. Note: China has strict import and sales regulations on e-cigarettes.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024); Tobacco Monopoly Law implementing regulations

short_description: CCC certification for 3D printers
description: 3D printers classified as IT equipment or industrial machinery may require CCC certification. Certain high-end 3D printers with specific capabilities (e.g., metal additive manufacturing) may also be subject to export controls.
source_citation: Announcement of CNCA on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: The applicable VAT rebate rate can be queried by exact HS Code via the official 2026A Tax Refund Rate Library.
description: China periodically updates its Export Tax Rebate Rate Library. The currently applicable version is 2026A, released by the State Taxation Administration in March 2026. Enterprises should download this library to verify the specific rebate rate corresponding to their product's precise 10-digit HS Code. Differences in the last digits of an HS Code can result in different rebate rates. Exporters should use the official offline tax refund software or online portal, load the library, and query their HS Code to see the exact rate.
source_citation: Announcement of the State Taxation Administration on Issuing the 2026A Version of the Export Rebate Rate Library

short_description: Check Dual-Use Items Control List
description: Certain drones, cameras, and sensors with specific capabilities may fall under China's Dual-Use Items Export Control List. Please verify against the full technical specifications in the list.
source_citation: Announcement of MOFCOM, GAC, etc. on the Dual-Use Items Export Control List, effective 2024.12.01
exemptions: Consumer-grade cameras with resolution below certain thresholds and no infrared/night vision capability are generally exempt. Chip designs at mature process nodes (28nm and above) for general commercial applications are generally exempt. Standard analog IC designs and power management IC designs typically do not trigger controls.
risk_scenarios: Cameras with AI-based object recognition or tracking capabilities, even if marketed as consumer products, may be reclassified as dual-use items. Exporting to military-end-users or for military use is prohibited regardless of technical specifications. Shipments to Hong Kong and Macau are subject to the same controls as international exports. Exporting GDSII design files for chips at 14nm and below to overseas foundries requires a technology export license, even if the design company is fabless. Chip designs incorporating AI/ML accelerators, high-speed SerDes (112Gbps+), or HBM interfaces may be classified as controlled technology. Design files for chips intended for military or surveillance applications are prohibited from export regardless of process node.

short_description: UAV temporary export controls apply
description: Certain Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and related equipment are subject to temporary export controls. If the drone is known to be used for military purposes, export is prohibited regardless of specifications.
source_citation: MOFCOM Announcements on UAV temporary export controls (2023, 2024)
exemptions: Toy drones under 250g without camera or data transmission capabilities are generally exempt.
risk_scenarios: Any drone capable of autonomous flight (beyond simple hover) or with flight time exceeding 30 minutes may require export license regardless of weight. Export to countries under UN arms embargo is prohibited. The 2026 Japan-specific controls add additional end-user screening requirements.

short_description: Consumer drones under 2kg still require UAV export-control pre-check
description: Consumer or commercial UAVs under 2kg may still require screening before export from China when they include cameras, GPS/autonomous flight, wireless video links, lithium batteries, or payload interfaces. Confirm whether the product falls within UAV temporary export controls, the Dual-Use Items Export Control List, end-user/end-use restrictions, SRRC/radio requirements, and lithium battery transport rules.
source_citation: MOFCOM and GAC UAV temporary export control announcements; Dual-Use Items Export Control List
exemptions: Toy drones under 250g without cameras, data transmission, payload interfaces, autonomous navigation, or military end-use indicators may be outside the higher-risk control scope.
risk_scenarios: A low-weight drone can still become controlled if it has long endurance, autonomous route planning, thermal or night-vision payloads, encrypted video transmission, or a restricted end user or destination.

short_description: Check Dual-Use controls for drone parts and accessories
description: Certain drone components — including high-precision gimbals, thermal/ infrared camera payloads, video transmission modules with long-range data links, and specified flight controllers — may fall under China's Dual-Use Items Export Control List or UAV temporary export controls. Please verify against the full technical specifications in the list.
source_citation: MOFCOM Announcements on Dual-Use Items Export Control List (2024) and UAV temporary export controls (2023, 2024)
exemptions: Standard flip-chip packaging and wire-bond packaging at mature process nodes are generally exempt. Chiplet integration using standard interconnects (UCIe) without advanced 3D stacking may not trigger additional controls.
risk_scenarios: 3D IC stacking technologies (hybrid bonding, through-silicon via arrays) may be classified as controlled manufacturing technologies. Chiplets integrating HBM memory stacks with logic dies may trigger dual-use controls due to the combined performance exceeding individual chip thresholds. Advanced packaging technologies that enable heterogeneous integration of chips from multiple foundries may face supply chain verification requirements.

short_description: Check if encryption triggers controls
description: If this product contains embedded encryption functionality (e.g., encrypted data storage, encrypted communication), you must check if it triggers China's commercial encryption import/export controls.
source_citation: Commercial Encryption Import License List and Export Control List, effective 2021.01.01
exemptions: Devices using encryption solely for user authentication (e.g., password protection) or digital signatures may be exempt. Wi-Fi WPA2/WPA3 for personal use is generally not considered controlled encryption.
risk_scenarios: Products with end-to-end encryption, VPN functionality, or encrypted data storage above 256-bit key length may require commercial encryption registration. Exporting such products without a valid encryption license can result in criminal penalties under China's Encryption Law.

short_description: China Energy Label required for power adapters
description: External power supplies, adapters, and chargers must comply with China Energy Label requirements and display the energy efficiency grade on the product label.
source_citation: Administrative Measures on Energy Efficiency Labeling (2020)

short_description: NMPA registration required for medical devices
description: Medical devices imported into or sold in China must be registered with the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA). Classification (Class I, II, or III) determines the registration pathway and timeline.
source_citation: Regulations on the Supervision and Administration of Medical Devices (2021 Revision)

description: FCC Part 15 Compliance
short_description: Optical transceivers require FCC certification for US market
summary: FCC Part 15 certification required for US export
details: Optical transceiver modules exported to the US must comply with FCC Part 15 regulations for radio frequency devices.

description: Laser Radiation Warning Labels
short_description: Optical modules require laser safety labels
summary: Laser safety labels required for optical modules
details: Optical transceiver modules with laser emitters must comply with laser safety standards and display appropriate warning labels per IEC 60825.

description: RoHS Compliance for Industrial Robots
short_description: Industrial robots and robotic arms require RoHS compliance
summary: RoHS compliance required for robotic equipment
details: Industrial robots must comply with RoHS Directive (Restriction of Hazardous Substances). This includes robotic arms, controllers, and collaborative robots.

description: CE Certification for Industrial Robots
short_description: Industrial robots require CE marking for EU market
summary: CE marking required for industrial robots exported to EU
details: Industrial robots exported to EU must carry CE marking indicating compliance with EU safety standards including Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC.

short_description: CCC certification for solar products
description: Solar inverters and certain solar products may require CCC certification when sold in China. Grid-connected inverters fall under Class 09 (IT Equipment).
source_citation: CNCA Announcement on CCC Product Catalog (2024)

short_description: Solar products VAT refund
description: Solar panels, inverters and related products qualify for export VAT refund at the standard rate. Check current MOFCOM announcements for exact rates.

short_description: Solar product import duties
description: Solar panels and related equipment have specific import duty rates. Most solar products enjoy reduced or zero tariffs under various trade agreements.

short_description: Solar panel safety standard
description: Solar PV modules must comply with IEC 61215 (crystalline silicon) or IEC 61646 (thin-film) standards for international market access.

short_description: UFLPA forced-labor supply chain review for solar imports
description: US imports of solar panels, photovoltaic modules, cells, and upstream polysilicon require forced-labor supply chain tracing. CBP may detain shipments where the importer cannot document that inputs are outside UFLPA forced-labor exposure.
source_citation: US CBP / DHS UFLPA enforcement guidance

short_description: Section 301 China tariff exposure for solar cells/modules
description: US-bound solar cells and photovoltaic modules of China origin may be subject to Section 301 additional duties. Confirm the 10-digit HTS line, origin, exclusion status, and duty rate before shipment.

short_description: Anti-dumping / countervailing duty exposure for solar imports
description: US imports of crystalline silicon photovoltaic cells and modules can be subject to anti-dumping and countervailing duty proceedings or orders. Screen supplier, country of origin, production route, and HTS line before entry.
source_citation: US Department of Commerce AD/CVD proceedings database

short_description: US-bound China-origin goods require HTS and Section 301 tariff screening
description: Exports from China to the United States should be screened against the 10-digit US HTS classification, Section 301 additional duty exposure, exclusion status, and country-of-origin documentation before shipment.
source_citation: USTR Section 301 China tariff actions / US HTS import review baseline

short_description: US destination screening for dual-use, encryption, and restricted-party exposure
description: US-bound electronics, drones, network equipment, semiconductor items, encryption-enabled devices, and advanced computing products should be screened for BIS export-control, Entity List, end-use, and re-export risk before shipment.
source_citation: US BIS Export Administration Regulations and Entity List screening baseline

short_description: EU market access screening for CE, RED, RoHS, and WEEE obligations
description: Electronics, wireless devices, chargers, batteries, smart hardware, PV equipment, and industrial equipment placed on the EU market require conformity review for CE marking, RED/EMC/LVD, RoHS, WEEE, and applicable product safety documentation.
source_citation: European Commission product compliance and market surveillance baseline

short_description: EU TARIC, battery, WEEE, and sustainability documentation review
description: EU-bound new-energy, battery, solar, electronics, and industrial products should be checked for TARIC classification, producer responsibility, battery documentation, WEEE registration, and sustainability reporting where applicable.
source_citation: EU TARIC and product sustainability compliance baseline

short_description: ASEAN route origin documentation and anti-circumvention review
description: Exports from China to ASEAN markets, especially Vietnam and Malaysia routes, should maintain origin documentation and value-added evidence to support legitimate trade routing and avoid transshipment or anti-circumvention challenges.

short_description: Vietnam / Malaysia solar routes require origin and anti-circumvention review
description: China-origin solar panels, photovoltaic modules, cells, inverters, or upstream inputs routed to or through Vietnam or Malaysia should preserve supplier, bill-of-materials, production-step, and origin evidence to address transshipment, anti-circumvention, and downstream buyer due-diligence questions.
source_citation: RCEP / ASEAN origin documentation and solar supply-chain review baseline

short_description: Russia-bound export sanctions, dual-use, and end-use screening
description: Russia-bound electronics, drones, network equipment, batteries, industrial robots, semiconductor items, and advanced computing products require enhanced sanctions, restricted-party, dual-use, and end-use review before shipment.
source_citation: MOFCOM / BIS / EU sanctions screening baseline

short_description: US-origin goods imported into China require origin, re-export, and restricted-party review
description: Imports into China from the United States should be screened for China customs declaration requirements, CCC/SRRC or sector approvals where applicable, US-origin export or re-export license conditions, restricted-party exposure, and origin documentation.
source_citation: GAC China import baseline / US BIS re-export compliance baseline

short_description: Taiwan-origin imports require origin, cross-strait, and controlled-technology review
description: Imports into China from Taiwan should verify origin documentation, China customs declaration requirements, cross-strait technology transfer controls, semiconductor or electronics licensing exposure, and product approvals such as CCC/SRRC where applicable.
source_citation: GAC China import baseline / cross-strait technology compliance baseline

short_description: Japan-origin imports require origin, export-license, and China import approval review
description: Imports into China from Japan should verify China customs declaration requirements, Japanese export-license conditions for controlled technology or precision equipment, origin documentation, and China market approvals such as CCC/SRRC where applicable.
source_citation: GAC China import baseline / Japan export-control compliance baseline

short_description: Korea-origin imports require origin, export-license, and China import approval review
description: Imports into China from South Korea should verify China customs declaration requirements, Korea-origin export-license conditions for semiconductors or advanced equipment, origin documentation, and China market approvals such as CCC/SRRC where applicable.
source_citation: GAC China import baseline / Korea export-control compliance baseline

short_description: Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval required
description: This product contains a Wi-Fi and/or Bluetooth radio transmitter, which requires a Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (SRRC) before sale or use in China.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval, effective 2019.03.01
exemptions: Devices with transmit power below 10mW may qualify for simplified SRRC procedures. Bluetooth-only devices under certain power thresholds may be exempt.
risk_scenarios: Wi-Fi 6E/7 devices using 6GHz band are currently NOT approved for SRRC in China. Exporting such devices without confirming frequency compliance may result in customs seizure. Devices with software-defined radio (SDR) capabilities face additional scrutiny.

short_description: SRRC approval required for smart watches and wearables
description: Smart watches, fitness trackers, and wearables with Bluetooth and/or Wi-Fi require SRRC radio approval.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (2019)

short_description: SRRC approval required for smart home devices
description: Smart home devices using Wi-Fi, Zigbee, Z-Wave, Bluetooth, or other wireless protocols require SRRC radio approval.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (2019)

short_description: SRRC approval required for gaming controllers
description: Wireless gaming controllers, joysticks, and gamepads using Bluetooth or other radio protocols require SRRC approval.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (2019)

short_description: SRRC approval required for wireless peripherals
description: Wireless keyboards, mice, and other wireless computer peripherals require SRRC approval for their radio transmitters.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (2019)

short_description: SRRC approval for VR/AR devices with wireless modules
description: VR headsets and AR glasses containing Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or other wireless communication modules require SRRC Type Approval before import or sale in China.
source_citation: MIIT Regulations on Radio Transmission Equipment Type Approval (2019)

description: Battery Management System Requirements
short_description: Energy storage systems require certified BMS
summary: BMS certification required for energy storage systems
details: Energy storage systems must have certified Battery Management Systems (BMS) ensuring safe operation, thermal management, and protection against overcharging/discharging.

description: UN3480 Lithium Battery Shipping
short_description: Lithium batteries require UN3480 dangerous goods documentation
summary: UN3480 documentation required for lithium battery shipments
details: Lithium-ion batteries and battery-powered energy storage systems require UN3480 dangerous goods classification and proper documentation for air and sea transport.

short_description: Advanced chips, semiconductor equipment, and related technologies are subject to China's strictest export controls.
description: China imposes comprehensive export controls on advanced semiconductor chips, manufacturing equipment, and related technologies. Key controlled items include: (1) Advanced logic chips (FinFET/GAA at 14nm and below); (2) High-bandwidth memory (HBM) and 3D DRAM; (3) GPU/AI accelerator chips above certain performance thresholds; (4) Semiconductor manufacturing equipment including ALD, ALE, and advanced etching machines; (5) Electronic Design Automation (EDA) software for advanced process nodes; (6) Chiplet and 3D IC integration technologies. The 2026 Dual-Use Items Export License Management Catalog consolidates these controls. Exporters must verify whether their specific chip products, equipment, or technologies require export licenses.
source_citation: 2026 Dual-Use Items and Technologies Export License Management Catalog; MOFCOM Announcements on Semiconductor Export Controls
exemptions: Consumer-grade microcontrollers (MCUs) at mature process nodes (28nm and above) for general commercial use are generally exempt. Chips with transistor count below 50 billion and no AI/ML acceleration capability may not trigger high-performance controls. Legacy memory chips (DDR3, LPDDR3 and below) are typically exempt.
risk_scenarios: Any AI training or inference chip above 4800 TOPS of FP8 performance may trigger export license requirements regardless of commercial designation. GPU/NPU chips with high-speed interconnects (NVLink, CXL 2.0+) face additional scrutiny. Chips designed for data center deployment or supercomputing clusters are classified as controlled items even if labeled as 'consumer products'. Exporting GDSII design files to overseas foundries for 14nm and below requires a separate technology export license.

checklist.task: Screen HBM / advanced DRAM against 2026 dual-use catalog thresholds
checklist.desc: Confirm whether the memory IC (HBM stack, 3D DRAM, or high-bandwidth module) triggers MOFCOM export license requirements before shipping to the United States.

checklist.task: Prepare end-user and end-use statements for US consignee
checklist.desc: Collect signed end-user certificates, commodity classification worksheets, and contract excerpts showing civilian end-use for controlled semiconductor shipments.

checklist.task: Verify GDSII / technology export license for overseas fab flows
checklist.desc: If design data leaves China for tape-out, confirm separate technology export licensing for sub-14nm logic or controlled memory IP.

short_description: Since 2026, China Customs requires 'country of wafer fabrication (foundry location)' declaration for all ICs under HS 8542.
description: Effective January 2026, China's updated 'Standard Declaration Directory for Import & Export Goods' introduced a new mandatory declaration element for all integrated circuits classified under HS Code 8542: the 'country of wafer fabrication (foundry location)'. This requirement applies to both imports and exports. Exporters must accurately declare where the chip's wafer was manufactured, as this information is used for supply chain security assessments and may affect export control classification. Misdeclaration of foundry origin can result in customs penalties for false declaration.
source_citation: General Administration of Customs Announcement on the 2026 Standard Declaration Directory for Import & Export Goods
exemptions: ICs fabricated at multiple foundries where the final process step was performed in China may declare China as origin. Chips that are only packaged or tested in a country (not fabricated there) do not need to declare that country as wafer origin.
risk_scenarios: Misdeclaring a TSMC-fabricated chip as 'SMIC-fabricated' (or vice versa) is treated as false declaration and can trigger supply chain security investigations. Chips fabricated at multiple foundries require careful documentation of which wafer fabrication step occurred at which location. Non-Chinese foundries may be subject to additional scrutiny if the chip is destined for a sensitive end-use.

checklist.task: Declare country of wafer fabrication on HS 8542 customs entry
checklist.desc: Populate the mandatory foundry-location field on export declarations; align with wafer invoices and fab flow certificates.

checklist.task: Retain multi-fab process step traceability pack
checklist.desc: Document which fabrication steps occurred at each foundry when wafers move between sites before export to the US.

short_description: China's 2026 supply chain security regulations impose special restrictions on semiconductor industry supply chain data collection and audits.
description: Under China's 2026 Regulations on Industrial and Supply Chain Security, the semiconductor industry is designated as a critical industry subject to enhanced supply chain protection. Key implications: (1) Foreign entities conducting semiconductor supply chain audits or mapping within China may face restrictions on data collection; (2) Chinese semiconductor companies may be prohibited from complying with foreign government requests for supply chain information (e.g., UFLPA-related inquiries); (3) Information about advanced process node capabilities, yield rates, and customer lists may be classified as sensitive supply chain data; (4) Chinese chip designers using overseas foundries must ensure they are not violating Chinese export controls on design files (GDSII data) for advanced chips.
source_citation: State Council Regulations on Industrial and Supply Chain Security, effective April 7, 2026
exemptions: Routine commercial orders of standard commodity chips (e.g., standard DRAM, NAND flash) without access to sensitive process or yield data are generally exempt. Supply chain mapping limited to first-tier suppliers' names and locations is generally permissible.
risk_scenarios: Foreign entities conducting detailed semiconductor supply chain audits (process node verification, yield rate data, customer lists) within China may be denied access under the 2026 Supply Chain Security Regulations. Chinese chip companies may face penalties if they comply with foreign government requests (e.g., U.S. UFLPA inquiries) to provide detailed supply chain information on Chinese semiconductor suppliers. Exporting advanced chip design files (GDSII) to overseas foundries without verifying compliance with Chinese export controls can result in both export violations and supply chain security violations.

checklist.task: Limit sensitive yield / customer data shared with foreign buyers
checklist.desc: Review what process-node, yield, and customer-list data can be disclosed to US customers or auditors without violating PRC supply chain security rules.

checklist.task: Document internal approval before responding to UFLPA-style inquiries
checklist.desc: Route foreign supply-chain questionnaires through legal/compliance review before releasing fab or subcontractor details.

short_description: Optical transceivers and modules may be subject to dual-use export controls.
description: China's dual-use export control regulations cover certain optical communication modules, particularly high-performance coherent optical transceivers used in data centers and telecommunications infrastructure. Exporters must verify whether their optical modules meet the technical thresholds that trigger export licensing requirements.
source_citation: 2026 Dual-Use Items and Technologies Export License Management Catalog
exemptions: Standard SFP/SFP+ modules for enterprise networking (data rates below 25Gbps per channel) are generally exempt. Modules using only standard NRZ modulation without coherent detection are typically not controlled.
risk_scenarios: Coherent optical modules above 100Gbps, optical modules with integrated DSP, and modules designed for data center interconnects exceeding 10km reach may trigger dual-use controls. Silicon photonics-based optical transceivers at 400G and above are subject to enhanced scrutiny. Exporting such modules to countries under UN arms embargo is prohibited regardless of technical specifications.

short_description: Special export controls apply to semiconductor-related exports to certain Japanese entities.
description: Under the 2026 Japan-specific export control measures, China has implemented enhanced scrutiny for semiconductor-related exports to designated Japanese entities. This includes advanced logic chips, EDA software, and semiconductor manufacturing equipment. Exporters must verify the end-user and end-use before shipping to Japan.
source_citation: MOFCOM Announcements on Japan-specific Export Controls (2026)
exemptions: Standard electronic components and general-purpose chips not on the controlled items list, shipped to non-military Japanese end-users not on the classified management list, are generally exempt from Japan-specific controls.
risk_scenarios: Under the 2026 Japan controls, 40 Japanese entities are under classified management with enhanced scrutiny for semiconductor-related exports. Exporting advanced logic chips, EDA software, or semiconductor manufacturing equipment to ANY military end-user in Japan is prohibited. Even routine chip shipments to Japanese trading companies may require end-user certification if the ultimate consignee is unknown or suspected to be a controlled entity.

short_description: China imposes targeted import bans, anti-dumping investigations, and enhanced supply chain reviews on certain foreign chips.
description: China has implemented multiple import restrictions on foreign semiconductors: (1) Targeted import bans on specific chips — e.g., NVIDIA H200 AI chips were banned from import in early 2026, followed by RTX Pro 6000D; (2) Anti-dumping investigations — in September 2025, MOFCOM initiated an anti-dumping investigation on imported analog chips (interface ICs, gate driver ICs) originating from the United States; (3) Supply chain security reviews —国家安全部 requires strict security reviews and traceability management for imported chips used in critical infrastructure; (4) Rules of origin for ICs — per the China Semiconductor Industry Association, the origin of an IC (whether packaged or unpackaged) is determined by the country of the wafer fabrication factory (foundry location), not the packaging/testing location. Importers must verify their chips are not subject to import bans and correctly declare the wafer fabrication origin.
source_citation: GAC Import Bans on Specific NVIDIA Chips (2026); MOFCOM Anti-Dumping Investigation Announcement (2025); CSIA IC Origin Rules (2026); 国家安全部 Critical Hardware Supply Chain Security Requirements
exemptions: Standard consumer-grade chips for general commercial use that are not subject to specific import bans or anti-dumping measures are generally exempt. Chips fabricated at foundries in non-sanctioned countries and used for non-sensitive applications typically face fewer restrictions.
risk_scenarios: Importing chips from manufacturers under specific GAC import bans (e.g., NVIDIA H200) will result in customs seizure. Misdeclaring the wafer fabrication country of origin can trigger supply chain security investigations. Chips imported for use in critical infrastructure without supplier security review may be rejected. Anti-dumping duties may apply retroactively to analog chips from the United States if the investigation results in affirmative determinations.

short_description: Export controls on high-performance AI inference chips (GPU/NPU) with FP8 >=4800 TOPS, HBM2e+, or NVLink/CXL 2.0+.
description: MOFCOM Announcement 2026 No.18 imposes export license requirements for AI inference chips meeting any of: FP8 >=4800 TOPS, integrated HBM2e+ memory, or support for NVLink/CXL 2.0+ interconnects for data center/supercomputing. Also controls GDSII design files for sub-14nm logic chips and 2.5D/3D IC integration equipment technical data. Exempts certified consumer electronics end products (e.g., smartphones, tablets) but controlled chips exported separately remain subject. Related HS codes: 8541.40, 8542.31, 8479.50, 8486.20.
exemptions: Certified consumer electronics end products (e.g., smartphones, tablets) are exempt, but controlled chips exported separately remain subject.
risk_scenarios: Exporters of AI accelerators, GPUs, or chips with HBM must verify if performance thresholds are met and apply for dual-use export license. Design houses transferring GDSII for sub-14nm to overseas foundries need license. Advanced packaging equipment technical data exports also controlled.

checklist.id: Technical classification::Check BAFA/EU dual-use classification
checklist.desc: Map HS code, technical parameters, software, and technology against EU Dual-Use Annex I and the German Export List.

checklist.desc: Collect end-use statement, consignee details, and sanctions/restricted-party screening evidence before export.

checklist.task: Confirm BAFA licence or general authorisation path
checklist.desc: If listed or red-flagged, confirm whether a BAFA individual licence, Union General Export Authorisation, or no-licence decision is available.

checklist.id: Product evidence::Prepare CE/RED/EMC/LVD/RoHS technical file
checklist.desc: Collect EU declaration of conformity, test reports, labels, user instructions, and importer/responsible-person details.

checklist.id: Market access::Check German WEEE and battery obligations
checklist.task: Check German WEEE and battery obligations
checklist.desc: Confirm producer registration, take-back, and battery documentation for electronic or energy-storage products.

checklist.id: Border readiness::Screen radio and electrical safety risks

checklist.id: Classification::Check Dutch strategic-goods status

checklist.id: End-use controls::Screen destination and end-use
checklist.desc: Retain end-use statements and restricted-party screening for sensitive electronics or semiconductor routes.

short_description: Advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment exported from the Netherlands may require national authorisation.
description: The Netherlands has national authorisation requirements for certain advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment and related technology exported to destinations outside the EU. Semiconductor tooling, measuring, inspection, lithography, and related controlled technologies should be screened before shipment.
content_en: Certain advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment and related technology exported from the Netherlands to destinations outside the EU are subject to a national authorisation requirement.
content_zh: 从荷兰向欧盟以外出口特定先进半导体制造设备及相关技术时，可能适用荷兰国家授权要求。

checklist.id: Technical classification::Screen semiconductor tooling scope
checklist.desc: Check whether lithography, measuring, inspection, or related technology is covered by Dutch national semiconductor export-control measures.

checklist.id: Authorisation::Confirm national export authorisation
checklist.desc: If covered, prepare the Dutch authorisation application and destination/end-use evidence before shipment.

short_description: Singapore export, transhipment, transit, brokering, or transfer of strategic goods requires SGCA permit screening.
description: Singapore controls strategic goods and technology under its Strategic Goods Control framework. Export, transhipment, transit, brokering, intangible transfer, or technology transfer involving controlled electronics, semiconductors, encryption, or dual-use items should be screened for permit and registration requirements.
content_en: Singapore exports, transhipments, transits, brokering, and transfers involving strategic goods or technology should be screened for Strategic Goods Control permit and registration requirements.
content_zh: 新加坡对战略物项和技术实施管制；出口、转运、过境、经纪、无形转移或技术转移均应筛查许可证和注册要求。

checklist.id: Permit screening::Check SGCA permit and registration needs
checklist.task: Check SGCA permit and registration needs
checklist.desc: Screen export, transhipment, transit, brokering, and technology-transfer activities against Singapore strategic-goods controls.

checklist.id: Documentation::Prepare TradeNet and end-use evidence
checklist.desc: Retain product classification, end-use, consignee, and permit documentation for controlled electronics or semiconductor routes.

short_description: Singapore import certificate / delivery verification may apply to strategic goods imported for declared end-use.
description: Singapore’s Import Certificate and Delivery Verification process may apply where a foreign exporting country requires assurance for strategic goods. Importers must obtain the import permit via TradeNet, declare the ICDV number and quantities, upload required documents, and obtain written approval before re-exporting ICDV goods.
content_en: Singapore ICDV may apply to strategic goods imported for declared end-use, with import permit, document upload, delivery verification, and re-export approval obligations.
content_zh: 新加坡 ICDV 可能适用于战略物项进口；进口商需通过 TradeNet 办理进口许可证、申报 ICDV 信息、上传单证，并在再出口前取得书面批准。

checklist.id: Import certificate::Confirm ICDV requirement
checklist.desc: If the exporter or foreign authority requires ICDV, obtain Singapore Import Certificate before shipment.

checklist.id: Permit filing::Upload import documents in TradeNet
checklist.desc: Declare ICDV number, quantities, invoice, airway bill/bill of lading, packing list, and approved ICDV in the import permit.

checklist.id: Re-export control::Do not re-export ICDV goods without approval
checklist.task: Do not re-export ICDV goods without approval
checklist.desc: Confirm Customs written approval before re-exporting goods imported under an Import Certificate.

short_description: Mexico-bound electronic equipment may require NOM safety certification before import or sale.
description: Imports into Mexico of electronic, electrical, power-supply, charger, battery, and smart hardware products should be checked for mandatory NOM compliance, including NOM-001-SCFI-2018 safety requirements for electronic equipment where applicable.
content_en: Mexico-bound electronic and electrical products may require mandatory NOM compliance and certification before import or sale, including NOM-001-SCFI-2018 where applicable.
content_zh: 进入墨西哥的电子、电气、电源、充电器、电池和智能硬件产品，应检查适用的强制性 NOM 合规和认证要求。
source_citation: Secretaría de Economía / SNICE NOM guidance

checklist.id: NOM scope::Check mandatory NOM coverage by HS code
checklist.task: Check mandatory NOM coverage by HS code
checklist.desc: Confirm whether the product falls under NOM-001-SCFI-2018 or another mandatory NOM before import.

checklist.id: Certification::Prepare NOM certificate and test evidence
checklist.task: Prepare NOM certificate and test evidence
checklist.desc: Collect accredited lab reports, certificate, Spanish labels, and importer documentation required for customs clearance.

checklist.id: Labeling::Check Spanish commercial information
checklist.desc: Verify product marking, safety warnings, voltage/frequency, importer details, and packaging labels before sale.

short_description: Mexico import entry requires HS classification, customs documentation, and NOM evidence where the tariff line is covered.
description: Mexican customs entry should confirm HS classification, valuation, importer registration, NOM applicability, and supporting documents. If a tariff line is subject to a mandatory NOM, the importer should be ready to demonstrate compliance at or before importation.
content_en: Mexico import entry should confirm HS classification, valuation, importer registration, NOM applicability, and supporting documents for covered tariff lines.
content_zh: 墨西哥进口申报应确认 HS 分类、完税价格、进口商资质、NOM 适用性和支持单证；若税号受强制 NOM 管理，应准备合规证明。

checklist.id: Entry data::Confirm Mexican HS and valuation data
checklist.task: Confirm Mexican HS and valuation data
checklist.desc: Align invoice, packing list, importer registration, HS code, origin, and customs valuation before entry.

checklist.id: NOM evidence::Attach NOM evidence if required
checklist.desc: If the tariff line is covered by a NOM, prepare certificate, exemption support, or applicable compliance document for customs.

short_description: US-bound radiofrequency devices require FCC equipment authorization and labeling review before marketing or import.
description: Radiofrequency devices marketed, imported, or sold in the United States should be checked for FCC equipment authorization, technical labeling, responsible-party information, user instructions, and supplier declaration or certification pathway before shipment.
content_en: US-bound RF and digital devices should be checked for FCC equipment authorization, labeling, responsible-party information, and technical compliance evidence before import or marketing.
content_zh: 进入美国市场的射频和数字设备应检查 FCC 设备授权、标签、责任方信息和技术合规资料。

checklist.id: FCC scope::Classify RF or digital device pathway
checklist.task: Classify RF or digital device pathway
checklist.desc: Confirm whether the device is intentional radiator, unintentional radiator, or exempt accessory and identify certification or SDoC route.

checklist.id: FCC evidence::Prepare test report and label
checklist.desc: Keep FCC test report, FCC ID or SDoC statement, user manual notices, and responsible-party information ready before marketing.

checklist.id: Import readiness::Align product model and grantee data
checklist.task: Align product model and grantee data
checklist.desc: Check model number, module integration, antenna changes, and grantee/responsible-party information against the shipment documents.

short_description: US-bound lithium battery products should be screened for CPSC safety and recall exposure.
description: Consumer products containing lithium-ion batteries, power banks, chargers, e-bikes, scooters, and energy-storage accessories entering the United States should be checked for CPSC safety guidance, applicable voluntary standards, overheating/fire risk, labeling, and recall history.
content_en: US-bound lithium battery consumer products should be checked for CPSC safety guidance, overheating/fire risk, labeling, voluntary standards, and recall exposure.
content_zh: 进入美国的锂电池消费品应检查 CPSC 安全指南、过热/火灾风险、标签、适用标准和召回风险。

checklist.id: Safety scope::Check CPSC recall and hazard history
checklist.task: Check CPSC recall and hazard history
checklist.desc: Search CPSC recalls for the product type, battery pack, charger, and model family before shipment.

checklist.id: Battery evidence::Collect battery safety documents
checklist.desc: Prepare cell/pack specifications, charger compatibility data, UN38.3, safety test reports, and warnings for lithium products.

checklist.id: Labeling::Verify consumer warnings and instructions
checklist.desc: Confirm English warnings, charging instructions, manufacturer/importer data, and disposal information before sale.

short_description: EU-bound consumer electronics require GPSR product-safety and Safety Gate recall exposure review.
description: Consumer electronics, chargers, batteries, smart hardware, and connected devices placed on the EU market should be checked for General Product Safety Regulation obligations, traceability, responsible economic operator, warnings/instructions, and Safety Gate alert exposure.
content_en: EU-bound consumer electronics should be checked for GPSR product-safety obligations, traceability, responsible economic operator, warnings, and Safety Gate alert exposure.
content_zh: 进入欧盟市场的消费电子应检查 GPSR 产品安全、可追溯性、责任经营者、警示说明和 Safety Gate 风险。

checklist.id: GPSR scope::Check product safety and traceability
checklist.desc: Confirm EU responsible economic operator, traceability data, safety warnings, instructions, and product-risk assessment.

checklist.id: Safety Gate::Search alert exposure
checklist.desc: Review Safety Gate alerts for similar chargers, battery products, wireless devices, or child-accessible electronics.

checklist.id: Technical file::Align CE and GPSR files
checklist.desc: Ensure CE/RED/EMC/LVD/RoHS evidence and GPSR safety documentation tell the same product story.

short_description: EU-bound batteries and battery-powered products require EU Battery Regulation and EPR review.
description: Batteries, battery packs, energy storage systems, EV chargers with battery modules, and battery-powered electronics placed on the EU market should be checked for EU Battery Regulation obligations, CE marking where applicable, labeling, producer responsibility, due diligence, and documentation requirements.
content_en: EU-bound batteries and battery-powered products should be checked for EU Battery Regulation obligations, labeling, producer responsibility, due diligence, and documentation requirements.
content_zh: 进入欧盟市场的电池和含电池产品应检查欧盟电池法规、标签、生产者责任、尽职调查和技术文件要求。

checklist.id: Battery scope::Classify battery category
checklist.desc: Identify portable, industrial, EV, LMT, or SLI battery category and map applicable EU obligations.

checklist.id: EPR::Confirm producer responsibility
checklist.desc: Confirm EU producer registration, take-back, labeling, and waste-battery documentation for the target member state.

checklist.id: Technical file::Prepare battery documentation
checklist.desc: Collect battery chemistry, capacity, safety test evidence, carbon/due-diligence data where applicable, and declaration documents.

short_description: Mexico-bound consumer electronics should be checked for Spanish commercial-information and safety labeling obligations.
description: Consumer electronics, chargers, batteries, and smart devices imported into Mexico should be checked for Spanish commercial information, importer details, warnings, NOM evidence, warranty/manual requirements, and product-specific safety labeling before customs clearance and sale.
content_en: Mexico-bound consumer electronics should be checked for Spanish commercial information, importer details, warnings, NOM evidence, warranty/manual requirements, and product-specific labeling.
content_zh: 进入墨西哥的消费电子应检查西班牙语商业信息、进口商信息、警示、NOM 证明、保修/说明书和产品标签。
source_citation: PROFECO / Secretaría de Economía NOM consumer information guidance

checklist.id: Labeling scope::Confirm Spanish label and manual
checklist.desc: Prepare Spanish commercial information, importer details, warnings, warranty, and user manual content before sale.

checklist.id: NOM evidence::Align label with NOM certificate
checklist.desc: Ensure model names, electrical ratings, importer data, and NOM certificates match the goods and invoice.

short_description: Germany-bound electrical and electronic equipment requires ElektroG/WEEE registration and take-back readiness.
description: Electrical and electronic equipment placed on the German market should be checked for ElektroG/WEEE producer registration with stiftung ear, authorised representative obligations for foreign producers, WEEE symbol, take-back/recycling duties, annual reporting, and battery-removability information where applicable.
content_en: Germany-bound EEE should be checked for ElektroG/WEEE registration with stiftung ear, authorised representative obligations, WEEE symbol, take-back/recycling duties, reporting, and battery-removability data.
content_zh: 进入德国市场的电子电气设备应检查 ElektroG/WEEE 注册、stiftung ear、授权代表、WEEE 标识、回收义务、年度报告和电池可拆卸信息。
source_citation: Umweltbundesamt / BMUV ElektroG and stiftung ear

checklist.id: ElektroG scope::Confirm EEE category and producer role
checklist.task: Confirm EEE category and producer role
checklist.desc: Determine whether the product is EEE and whether the exporter/importer is the first party placing it on the German market.

checklist.id: stiftung ear::Check registration or authorised representative
checklist.desc: Confirm stiftung ear registration or authorised representative setup before shipment or online sale to Germany.

checklist.id: WEEE evidence::Prepare label and recycling documents
checklist.desc: Verify WEEE symbol, take-back information, battery removability, and reporting data for German market placement.

short_description: Germany-bound batteries and battery-powered products require German battery producer-responsibility review.
description: Batteries, accumulators, battery packs, power banks, energy storage systems, and products containing batteries placed on the German market should be checked for BattDG/Battery Regulation obligations, stiftung ear battery registration, producer responsibility organisation participation, take-back, labeling, and documentation requirements.
content_en: Germany-bound batteries and battery-powered products should be checked for BattDG/EU Battery Regulation obligations, stiftung ear registration, producer responsibility, take-back, labeling, and documentation.
content_zh: 进入德国市场的电池和含电池产品应检查 BattDG/欧盟电池法规、stiftung ear 注册、生产者责任组织、回收、标签和技术文件要求。
source_citation: Umweltbundesamt BattDG / stiftung ear battery obligations

checklist.id: Battery category::Classify battery type
checklist.desc: Identify whether the product contains portable, industrial, LMT, EV, or other battery categories and map German obligations.

checklist.id: Registration::Confirm stiftung ear battery registration
checklist.desc: Check whether a German battery producer registration or authorised representative is required before market placement.

checklist.id: Take-back::Prepare take-back and labeling evidence
checklist.desc: Prepare battery labeling, collection/take-back participation, safety and chemical documentation, and reporting data.

short_description: Germany-bound packaged goods may require Packaging Act/LUCID registration and system participation.
description: Goods sold or shipped into Germany with sales, transport, or product packaging should be checked for VerpackG obligations, LUCID packaging-register status, system participation, data reporting, and packaging-weight records. This is especially relevant for direct-to-consumer electronics and replacement parts.
content_en: Germany-bound packaged goods should be checked for VerpackG/LUCID registration, system participation, data reporting, and packaging-weight records.
content_zh: 发往德国的带包装商品应检查 VerpackG/LUCID 注册、回收系统参与、数据申报和包装重量记录。

checklist.id: Packaging scope::Check LUCID registration need
checklist.desc: Confirm whether sales packaging, shipment packaging, or direct-to-consumer sales trigger German Packaging Act obligations.

checklist.id: System participation::Prepare packaging records
checklist.desc: Keep packaging material weights, system participation records, LUCID registration number, and annual data reporting support.

short_description: Netherlands-bound electrical and electronic equipment requires WEEE/EPR registration and reporting review.
description: Electrical and electronic devices produced, imported, sold, or directly supplied to Dutch end users should be checked for Dutch WEEE/EPR obligations, registration/reporting, WEEE symbol, collection/recycling duties, battery removability information, and Stichting OPEN or relevant scheme participation.
content_en: Netherlands-bound EEE should be checked for WEEE/EPR obligations, registration/reporting, WEEE symbol, collection/recycling duties, battery removability information, and relevant scheme participation.
content_zh: 进入荷兰市场的电子电气设备应检查 WEEE/EPR、注册/报告、WEEE 标识、回收义务、电池可拆卸信息和相关组织参与。
source_citation: Business.gov.nl electrical and electronic equipment EPR

checklist.id: WEEE scope::Confirm Dutch producer responsibility
checklist.desc: Check whether the company first markets, imports, or sells EEE directly to Dutch end users.

checklist.id: Registration::Prepare EPR registration and reporting
checklist.desc: Prepare registration, reporting, WEEE symbol, battery removability and recycling documentation.

short_description: Netherlands-bound batteries and battery-containing products require Dutch battery EPR review.
description: Batteries, accumulators, battery packs, power banks, energy-storage products, and products containing batteries placed on the Dutch market should be checked for Dutch battery EPR, collection/recycling, notification, take-back, consumer information, and reporting obligations.
content_en: Netherlands-bound batteries and battery-containing products should be checked for Dutch battery EPR, collection/recycling, notification, take-back, consumer information, and reporting obligations.
content_zh: 进入荷兰市场的电池及含电池产品应检查电池 EPR、收集回收、通知、回收、消费者信息和报告义务。

checklist.id: Battery EPR::Confirm first-market role
checklist.desc: Check whether the importer, brand owner, trader, or foreign seller has Dutch battery EPR obligations.

checklist.id: Battery reporting::Prepare collection and consumer information
checklist.desc: Prepare notification/reporting data, take-back arrangements, consumer information, and battery documentation.

short_description: Netherlands-bound packaged goods may trigger packaging waste contribution and annual reporting obligations.
description: Importers or producers placing packaging material on the Dutch market should check packaging waste management contribution thresholds, Verpact registration, annual packaging-weight reporting, and indirect export refund records, especially for electronics and spare parts shipped in consumer packaging.
content_en: Netherlands-bound packaged goods may trigger packaging waste contribution, Verpact registration, annual packaging-weight reporting, and indirect export refund records.
content_zh: 进入荷兰市场的带包装商品可能触发包装废弃物缴费、Verpact 注册、年度包装重量申报和间接出口退费记录要求。

checklist.id: Packaging threshold::Check Dutch packaging threshold
checklist.desc: Confirm whether packaging quantities exceed the Dutch contribution/reporting threshold and whether Verpact registration is required.

checklist.id: Records::Keep packaging-weight records
checklist.desc: Maintain packaging material, quantity, annual report, contribution, and indirect export refund evidence where relevant.

short_description: Singapore-bound telecom and wireless equipment require IMDA import and equipment-registration review.
description: Telecommunication equipment imported into Singapore should be screened for IMDA dealer licensing, equipment registration before local sale or hire, TradeNet permit declarations, AHTN/product-code requirements, prohibited-equipment prior approval, and compliance-label obligations.
content_en: Singapore-bound telecom and wireless equipment should be screened for IMDA dealer licensing, equipment registration, TradeNet permit declarations, prior approval for prohibited equipment, and compliance-label obligations.
content_zh: 进入新加坡的电信和无线设备应检查 IMDA 经销商许可、设备注册、TradeNet 申报、禁止设备预先批准和合规标签要求。
source_citation: IMDA Requirement for Import of Telecommunication Equipment

checklist.id: IMDA scope::Confirm telecom equipment registration path
checklist.desc: Check whether the product connects to telecom networks, uses radio spectrum, or falls under IMDA equipment-registration classes.

checklist.id: TradeNet::Check AHTN and product-code declaration
checklist.desc: Confirm AHTN code, IMDA product code, dealer licence, and prior approval need before import declaration.

checklist.id: Labeling::Prepare IMDA compliance label evidence
checklist.desc: Verify equipment registration record, compliance label, model number, and user documentation before local sale or hire.

short_description: Singapore-controlled household electrical and electronic products may require SAFETY Mark registration before sale.
description: Controlled goods under Singapore Consumer Protection (Safety Requirements) Regulations should be checked for CPSO/Enterprise Singapore registration, valid SAFETY Mark, accredited test report, model coverage, supplier obligations, and online listing compliance before import or sale.
content_en: Singapore controlled goods should be checked for CPSO/Enterprise Singapore registration, valid SAFETY Mark, accredited test report, model coverage, supplier obligations, and online listing compliance.
content_zh: 新加坡受控商品应检查 CPSO/Enterprise Singapore 注册、SAFETY Mark、认可测试报告、型号覆盖、供应商义务和线上销售合规。
source_citation: Singapore Consumer Product Safety Regulations / CPSO

checklist.id: Controlled goods::Check SAFETY Mark scope
checklist.desc: Confirm whether the product is a controlled good requiring registration and SAFETY Mark before sale in Singapore.

checklist.id: Registration::Prepare registered supplier and test evidence
checklist.task: Prepare registered supplier and test evidence
checklist.desc: Collect registered supplier details, valid test report, certificate, model list, and SAFETY Mark artwork.

checklist.id: Online sale::Review marketplace listing compliance
checklist.desc: Make sure online listings show correct model, registration status, warnings, and SAFETY Mark where required.

short_description: Wi-Fi routers sold in Singapore require CSA Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme review in addition to IMDA registration.
description: Wi-Fi routers intended for local sale or use in Singapore should be checked for IMDA TS RG-SEC and CSA Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme Level 1 minimum requirements, cybersecurity label, equipment registration, software/security update commitments, and consumer-facing security information.
content_en: Wi-Fi routers sold in Singapore should be checked for IMDA TS RG-SEC and CSA Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme Level 1 minimum requirements, cybersecurity label, equipment registration, and security update commitments.
content_zh: 在新加坡销售的 Wi-Fi 路由器应检查 IMDA TS RG-SEC、CSA Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme Level 1、网络安全标签、设备注册和安全更新承诺。
source_citation: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore CLS registration

checklist.id: CLS scope::Confirm Wi-Fi router CLS requirement
checklist.desc: Check whether the device is a Wi-Fi router sold for local use and subject to CSA CLS / IMDA TS RG-SEC.

checklist.id: Cybersecurity evidence::Prepare CLS submission data
checklist.desc: Prepare product security information, firmware update policy, default password controls, vulnerability handling process, and label evidence.

short_description: Singapore-bound energy storage systems require SCDF Fire Code and installation-safety review.
description: Energy storage systems, battery rooms, and large lithium battery installations for Singapore should be checked for SCDF Fire Code requirements, setback/compartmentation, fire protection, certification, installation approval, operation and maintenance controls, and site-specific fire-safety assessment.
content_en: Singapore-bound ESS and battery-room installations should be checked for SCDF Fire Code requirements, setback/compartmentation, fire protection, certification, installation approval, O&M controls, and site-specific fire-safety assessment.
content_zh: 进入新加坡的储能系统和电池房安装应检查 SCDF Fire Code、间距/防火分隔、消防保护、认证、安装批准、运维控制和现场消防评估。
source_citation: Singapore MHA / SCDF Fire Safety Requirements for ESS

checklist.id: ESS scope::Confirm SCDF Fire Code applicability
checklist.desc: Identify ESS capacity, chemistry, indoor/outdoor installation, battery-room layout, and Fire Code trigger points.

checklist.id: Site approval::Prepare fire-safety design evidence
checklist.desc: Prepare certification, fire protection design, setback/compartment details, emergency response and O&M procedures.

short_description: Mexico-bound telecom, radio, and wireless equipment require IFT homologation and IFT mark review.
description: Products, equipment, devices, or apparatus for telecommunications or broadcasting that connect to a telecommunications network or use radio spectrum in Mexico should be checked for IFT homologation certificate, conformity assessment, technical provisions, homologation number, Sello IFT marking, importer use of the mark, and certificate validity.
content_en: Mexico-bound telecom, radio, and wireless equipment should be checked for IFT homologation certificate, conformity assessment, technical provisions, homologation number, Sello IFT marking, importer mark use, and certificate validity.
content_zh: 进入墨西哥的电信、广播、无线和射频设备应检查 IFT homologation 证书、符合性评估、技术规定、 homologation 编号、Sello IFT 标识、进口商使用标识和证书有效性。
source_citation: IFT Homologación y Evaluación de la Conformidad

checklist.id: IFT scope::Confirm telecom/radio homologation trigger
checklist.desc: Check whether the product connects to telecom networks or uses radio spectrum and requires IFT homologation.

checklist.id: Certificate::Prepare homologation certificate and mark
checklist.desc: Verify certificate holder, model list, technical provision, homologation number, Sello IFT marking, and importer use of the mark.

short_description: Mexico-bound electronic, electrical, and household products require NOM-024 commercial-information review.
description: Electronic, electrical, and household appliance products imported into Mexico should be checked for NOM-024-SCFI commercial-information requirements covering packaging, instructions, warranty, importer/manufacturer details, model identification, electrical ratings, warnings, and Spanish-language consumer information.
content_en: Mexico-bound electronic, electrical, and household appliance products should be checked for NOM-024-SCFI commercial-information requirements covering packaging, instructions, warranty, importer/manufacturer details, model identification, ratings, warnings, and Spanish-language consumer information.
content_zh: 进入墨西哥的电子、电气和家电产品应检查 NOM-024-SCFI 商业信息要求，包括包装、说明书、保修、进口商/制造商信息、型号、电气参数、警示和西班牙语消费者信息。

checklist.id: NOM-024 scope::Confirm commercial information requirement
checklist.desc: Check whether packaging, instructions, warranty, and consumer information must follow NOM-024-SCFI.

checklist.id: Spanish evidence::Prepare label/manual/warranty package
checklist.desc: Prepare Spanish labels, manual, warranty, importer/manufacturer details, model data, ratings, and warnings before import or sale.

short_description: Mexico import entries require TIGIE tariff classification and NICO statistical suffix review.
description: Imports into Mexico should confirm TIGIE tariff fraction, NICO statistical code, HS classification support, applicable NOM/IFT restrictions, importer registry status, valuation, origin, and customs-document consistency before filing the pedimento.
content_en: Mexico import entries should confirm TIGIE tariff fraction, NICO statistical code, HS support, applicable NOM/IFT restrictions, importer registry, valuation, origin, and customs-document consistency before filing the pedimento.
content_zh: 墨西哥进口申报应确认 TIGIE 税号、NICO 统计代码、HS 分类依据、适用 NOM/IFT 限制、进口商登记、完税价格、原产地和单证一致性。
source_citation: SNICE / ANAM import and tariff classification guidance

checklist.id: Tariff line::Confirm TIGIE and NICO code
checklist.desc: Validate Mexican tariff fraction and NICO suffix against product specs, BOM, and customs broker classification support.

checklist.id: Pedimento::Align customs entry data
checklist.desc: Align invoice, origin, valuation, importer registry, applicable NOM/IFT controls, and pedimento data before entry.

short_description: Japan-bound wireless and radio equipment require MIC Radio Law technical conformity review.
description: Wireless, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cellular, and radio equipment imported or marketed in Japan should be checked for Radio Law technical regulations conformity certification, construction design certification where applicable, Giteki mark display, model coverage, antenna/radio module conditions, and importer documentation.
content_en: Japan-bound wireless and radio equipment should be checked for MIC Radio Law technical conformity certification, Giteki mark display, model coverage, antenna/module conditions, and importer documentation.
content_zh: 进入日本市场的无线和射频设备应检查 MIC Radio Law 技术基准适合证明、技适标志、型号覆盖、天线/无线模块条件和进口商文件。
source_citation: MIC Japan Radio Use / Technical Regulations Conformity Certification

checklist.id: Radio scope::Confirm Radio Law conformity trigger
checklist.desc: Check whether the product includes Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cellular, or other radio transmitters subject to MIC technical regulations.

checklist.id: Giteki evidence::Prepare mark and certificate coverage
checklist.desc: Verify certificate holder, model list, module/antenna conditions, Giteki mark placement, user manual notes, and importer records.

short_description: Japan-bound electrical appliances, chargers, adapters, and lithium-ion batteries may require PSE review.
description: Electrical appliances and materials imported or sold in Japan should be checked under METI Electrical Appliance and Material Safety Act requirements for PSE marking, specified/non-specified product category, conformity assessment, importer notification, inspection records, labels, and lithium-ion battery coverage where applicable.
content_en: Japan-bound electrical appliances, chargers, adapters, and lithium-ion batteries should be checked for PSE marking, conformity assessment, importer notification, inspection records, and labels.

short_description: MOFCOM announced a preliminary affirmative determination in an anti-dumping investigation covering interface ICs and gate driver ICs under HS headings 8542 and
description: MOFCOM announced a preliminary affirmative determination in an anti-dumping investigation covering interface ICs and gate driver ICs under HS headings 8542 and 8541. Exporters should review country-specific duties when shipping to China.
content_en: MOFCOM announced a preliminary affirmative determination in an anti-dumping investigation covering interface ICs and gate driver ICs under HS headings 8542 and 8541. Exporters should review country-specific duties when shipping to China.
content_zh: 商务部公布了对原产于美国的某些模拟集成电路（包括接口IC和栅极驱动IC，HS编码8542和8541）反倾销调查的初步肯定性裁定。出口商在向中国发货时应审查国别特定关税。

short_description: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) reporting obligations may apply to steel-aluminum embodied exports, including electronics enclosures and heat sinks
description: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) reporting obligations may apply to steel-aluminum embodied exports, including electronics enclosures and heat sinks with aluminum content above threshold levels. Ensure compliance with CBAM reporting requirements for EU-bound shipments.
content_en: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) reporting obligations may apply to steel-aluminum embodied exports, including electronics enclosures and heat sinks with aluminum content above threshold levels. Ensure compliance with CBAM reporting requirements for EU-bound shipments.
content_zh: 欧盟碳边境调节机制（CBAM）报告义务可能适用于含钢铝的出口产品，包括铝含量超过阈值的电子外壳和散热器。确保对欧盟出口的货物符合CBAM报告要求。

short_description: Applied Materials agreed to pay $252 million penalty for illegally exporting semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China, violating BIS export controls. This
description: Applied Materials agreed to pay $252 million penalty for illegally exporting semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China, violating BIS export controls. This case highlights the critical need for robust compliance with US export regulations, including Entity List screening and end-use checks.
content_en: Applied Materials agreed to pay $252 million penalty for illegally exporting semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China, violating BIS export controls. This case highlights the critical need for robust compliance with US export regulations, including Entity List screening and end-use checks.
content_zh: 应用材料公司因非法向中国出口半导体制造设备，违反BIS出口管制，同意支付2.52亿美元罚款。此案凸显了严格遵守美国出口法规、包括实体清单筛查和最终用途检查的紧迫性。

short_description: The US Department of Commerce has revised its license review policy for semiconductors exported to China, tightening controls on advanced chips and related equi
description: The US Department of Commerce has revised its license review policy for semiconductors exported to China, tightening controls on advanced chips and related equipment. Exporters must now demonstrate that shipments do not involve entities on the BIS Entity List or contribute to China's military modernization. The policy aligns with Section 301 tariff exposure and requires FCC Part 15/18 compliance for wireless-capable semiconductors.
content_en: The US Department of Commerce has revised its license review policy for semiconductors exported to China, tightening controls on advanced chips and related equipment. Exporters must now demonstrate that shipments do not involve entities on the BIS Entity List or contribute to China's military modernization. The policy aligns with Section 301 tariff exposure and requires FCC Part 15/18 compliance for wireless-capable semiconductors.
content_zh: 美国商务部修订了对华半导体出口的许可证审查政策，加强了对先进芯片及相关设备的管控。出口商必须证明货物不涉及BIS实体清单上的实体，也不会助力中国军事现代化。该政策与301条款关税风险挂钩，并要求具备无线功能的半导体符合FCC Part 15/18标准。

short_description: The Department of Commerce rescinded a Biden-era firearms rule, restoring standard export controls for civilian firearms. This reduces licensing burdens for com
description: The Department of Commerce rescinded a Biden-era firearms rule, restoring standard export controls for civilian firearms. This reduces licensing burdens for commercial exports to the US, but exporters must still comply with baseline BIS regulations and avoid restricted parties.
content_en: The Department of Commerce rescinded a Biden-era firearms rule, restoring standard export controls for civilian firearms. This reduces licensing burdens for commercial exports to the US, but exporters must still comply with baseline BIS regulations and avoid restricted parties.
content_zh: 美国商务部撤销了拜登时期的枪支管制规则，恢复了对民用枪支的标准出口管制。这减轻了向美国商业出口的许可负担，但出口商仍需遵守BIS基本规定，避免涉及受限方。

short_description: Commerce Eases Export Controls on Syria. This change may reduce licensing requirements for certain items, but US export controls remain stringent for dual-use g
description: Commerce Eases Export Controls on Syria. This change may reduce licensing requirements for certain items, but US export controls remain stringent for dual-use goods. Ensure compliance with BIS regulations and Entity List screening.
content_en: Commerce Eases Export Controls on Syria. This change may reduce licensing requirements for certain items, but US export controls remain stringent for dual-use goods. Ensure compliance with BIS regulations and Entity List screening.
content_zh: 美国商务部放宽对叙利亚的出口管制。此变更可能减少某些物项的许可要求，但美国对两用物项的出口管制仍然严格。确保遵守BIS法规并进行实体清单筛查。
